article_meta.title: President Donald Trump dismissed sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as “disgusting” and decried his procurer, Ghislaine Maxwel

transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective included

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: President Donald Trump dismissed sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as “disgusting” and decried his procurer, Ghislaine Maxwel
SOURCE: Breitbart Fact Check

ARTICLE:

President Donald Trump dismissed sex offender Jeffrey Epstein as “disgusting” and decried his procurer, Ghislaine Maxwell as “evil” back in 2006, a now-retired cop informed FBI agents in 2019. The revelation is contained in a newly unsealed court document released Monday by the Department of Justice. CNBC reports Trump personally called the then-Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter “to tell him ‘thank goodness you’ [are] stopping [Epstein], everyone has known he’s been doing this,” Reiter told the FBI in October 2019, according to the FBI document, known as a 302. The document stated whistleblower Trump “was one of the very first people to call” the police when word spread Epstein was under investigation. This simple fact defies the president’s opponents who have since spread lies and falsehoods about his relationship with Epstein and convicted associate Maxwell. Trump said in the call he distanced himself from Epstein, according to the document, claiming he “got the hell out of there” when around Epstein with teenagers present. The outlet notes, “Reiter’s name is redacted from the 302. But the document identifies the interview subject as the person who had been Palm Beach’s police chief at the time of the department’s investigation of Epstein, who was Reiter.” Reiter told the Miami Herald, which first reported the document, Trump called him in 2006, after the police department’s probe of Epstein became publicly known. Trump told Reiter he had thrown Epstein out of his club, Mar-a-Lago, which is located in Palm Beach, the summary said. RELATED: RELEASE THE FILES! Trump Says “The Democrats Were Epstein’s Friends” “Trump told him [Reiter] people in New York knew Epstein was disgusting,” according to the FBI’s 302 summary of its interview with Reiter. “Trump said Maxwell was Epstein’s operative, ‘she is evil and to focus on her,’ ” the FBI said in the 302. The document came to light hours after Maxwell’s lawyer called on Trump to grant her executive clemency so she could speak “honestly” about what she knows. Maxwell, earlier Monday, repeatedly refused to testify to a House committee. Trump has consistently denied any wrongdoing in relation to Epstein, with whom he says he severed contact decades ago, and has not been accused of any crimes by Epstein’s victims. Follow Simon Kent on Twitter: Follow @SunSimonKent or e-mail to: skent@breitbart.com
---

{
  "summary": "A newly unsealed FBI document reveals that in 2006, then-businessman Donald Trump contacted Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter to criticize sex offender Jeffrey Epstein and his associate, Ghislaine Maxwell. According to the document, Trump called Epstein 'disgusting' and Maxwell 'evil.' Reiter, in a 2019 FBI interview, said Trump was among the first to reach out after public news of the Epstein investigation, expressing support and relief that authorities were pursuing Epstein. Trump affirmed he had distanced himself from Epstein, even removing him from his Mar-a-Lago resort after observing Epstein's inappropriate behavior with teenagers. The FBI document, partially redacted, identifies Reiter as the police chief consulted at the time. The newly revealed details run contrary to opponents' narratives about Trump's ties to Epstein and Maxwell. The document’s release coincided with public statements from Maxwell’s lawyers urging Trump to grant her clemency, and revelations that Maxwell has refused to testify before Congress. Trump has repeatedly denied any wrongdoing regarding his association with Epstein, stating that their relationship ended years prior and no accusations have been made against him by Epstein’s victims.",
  "key_facts": [
    "Trump called Epstein 'disgusting' and Maxwell 'evil' in a 2006 phone call to police chief Michael Reiter.",
    "FBI documents indicate Trump was one of the first to support police investigating Epstein.",
    "Trump claims he distanced himself from Epstein and removed him from Mar-a-Lago after witnessing inappropriate behavior.",
    "Details contradict narratives about Trump having close ties to Epstein and Maxwell.",
    "The document emerged as Maxwell's lawyers asked Trump for executive clemency and she refused to testify before Congress."
  ],
  "sources_represented": ["Breitbart", "CNBC", "Miami Herald"]
}

parsed_output.summary: A newly unsealed FBI document reveals that in 2006, then-businessman Donald Trump contacted Palm Beach Police Chief Michael Reiter to criticize sex offender Jeffrey Epstein and his associate, Ghislaine Maxwell. According to the document, Trump called Epstein 'disgusting' and Maxwell 'evil.' Reiter, in a 2019 FBI interview, said Trump was among the first to reach out after public news of the Epstein investigation, expressing support and relief that authorities were pursuing Epstein. Trump affirmed he had distanced himself from Epstein, even removing him from his Mar-a-Lago resort after observing Epstein's inappropriate behavior with teenagers. The FBI document, partially redacted, identifies Reiter as the police chief consulted at the time. The newly revealed details run contrary to opponents' narratives about Trump's ties to Epstein and Maxwell. The document’s release coincided with public statements from Maxwell’s lawyers urging Trump to grant her clemency, and revelations that Maxwell has refused to testify before Congress. Trump has repeatedly denied any wrongdoing regarding his association with Epstein, stating that their relationship ended years prior and no accusations have been made against him by Epstein’s victims.
key_facts: Trump called Epstein 'disgusting' and Maxwell 'evil' in a 2006 phone call to police chief Michael Reiter.
FBI documents indicate Trump was one of the first to support police investigating Epstein.
Trump claims he distanced himself from Epstein and removed him from Mar-a-Lago after witnessing inappropriate behavior.
Details contradict narratives about Trump having close ties to Epstein and Maxwell.
The document emerged as Maxwell's lawyers asked Trump for executive clemency and she refused to testify before Congress.
